title: to-do
text:
The GitHub repo IOsinnii/personal-blog is a fork of Samantha Csik’s (samanthacsik.github.io) full personal website — a fairly complex Quarto site with many pages, custom SCSS styling, custom fonts (Nunito + Josefin Sans), FontAwesome icons, a logo image, and lots of Samantha-specific content.
The existing blog-iosinnii folder in the workspace is an unrelated fresh Quarto blog template (default starter, not the fork). We’ll clone the actual fork to a new location.




Before implementing, we’ll need: 1. Your name — to replace “Sam Shanny-Csik” throughout the site 2. Your social links — GitHub username, LinkedIn URL, email address 3. Sections to keep — Samantha’s site has: home, about, talks & workshops, courses, projects, blog (posts), resources. Which ones do you actually want? A minimal personal blog might just be: home, about, blog, projects. 4. Style preferences — Keep the green/sage palette and restyle, or swap to a different palette/font entirely?
We can start with Stage 1 right away and gather this info as we go.




What: Clone https://github.com/IOsinnii/personal-blog.git into a new local folder.
Steps: - Run git clone https://github.com/IOsinnii/personal-blog.git in C:\Users\osenn\Documents\R\ - This creates C:\Users\osenn\Documents\R\personal-blog\ - Open that folder as an RStudio project (File → Open Project) - Note: the existing blog-iosinnii folder is a separate starter template — it can stay or be deleted




What: Strip out all of Samantha’s identity, links, tracking, and personal content. Replace with placeholders for your info.





Change title: "Sam Shanny-Csik" → your name
Remove site-url: https://samanthacsik.github.io/
Remove google-analytics block (tracking ID)
Remove favicon: images/favicon/light-sage-chick.png (or replace with a new one)
Replace logo: and logo-alt: (currently Samantha’s logo image)
Update navbar social links in the “get in touch!” dropdown (LinkedIn, email)
Update href: https://github.com/samanthacsik/... → your GitHub URL
Update page-footer copyright: © 2021 Samantha Shanny-Csik → your name and year





Replace image: images/sam_cropped.jpg → your profile photo (or placeholder)
Replace bio text (“Hi there! I’m Sam Shanny-Csik…”) → your intro
Replace social icon links (LinkedIn, GitHub, Bluesky, email)





This is the most content-heavy page. Replace entirely with a simple placeholder “about me” section. The current content includes Samantha’s full bio, Strava activity map (R code that requires AWS credentials), personal photos, a Hungarian recipe, and her logo story — all of which needs to go.
We’ll write a clean placeholder template you can fill in later.





Remove all of Samantha’s blog posts (they reference her teaching, specific courses, etc.)
Add one placeholder “hello world” starter post





Pending your decision on which sections to keep (see questions above)
At minimum, clear the content and replace with placeholder text





Delete Samantha’s personal photos, logo files, favicon
Replace with placeholder or your own images





Rename to personal-blog.Rproj





Delete (this is Samantha’s pre-built HTML output; it will be regenerated by Quarto)







What: Trim the navbar to a simpler structure appropriate for a personal blog.
Samantha’s current navbar: > home | about | talks & workshops | courses | projects | posts | get in touch | GitHub
Proposed minimal structure (adjust to taste): > home | about | blog | projects | GitHub
Steps: - Remove talks_workshops.qmd, courses.qmd, resources.qmd from the navbar in _quarto.yml - Optionally delete those .qmd files and their corresponding folders




What: Update the visual identity. The current styles.scss uses: - Fonts: Nunito (body) + Josefin Sans (headings) — these are nice and worth keeping, or swap - Colors: sage green palette + terracotta accents on a cream background
Proposed approach: - Keep the SCSS structure (it’s well-organized and has useful utility classes) - Swap the color variables at the top of styles.scss to a different palette of your choice - Optionally change mainfont in _quarto.yml and the @import line in styles.scss - Remove Samantha-specific CSS that won’t apply (e.g., .navbar-brand img logo sizing, .rounded headshot shape for her specific layout)




What: Render the site locally and check for any remaining references to Samantha.
Steps: - Run quarto render in the project directory - Search all .qmd files for any remaining occurrences of “Samantha”, “csik”, “scsik@”, “samanthacsik”, “strava”, etc. - Check rendered output in a browser - Commit the cleaned state to Git







Step
Action
Effort




1
Clone repo
Low


2a
Update _quarto.yml
Low


2b
Replace index.qmd
Low


2c
Replace about.qmd
Medium


2d
Clean posts/
Low


2e
Clean projects/ etc.
Low–Medium


2f
Clean images/
Low


2g
Rename .Rproj, delete docs/
Low


3
Simplify navbar
Low


4
Restyle styles.scss
Medium


5
Render + verify
Low



We’ll work through these in small sessions as you requested. Stage 1 (clone) and Stage 2a–2b (config + home page) are a natural first session.

title: Post With Code
text:
This is a post with executable code.

1 + 1

[1] 2

title: How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
text:
By default, _quarto.yml uses css: styles.css. For serious styling, switch to an SCSS overlay — it lets you override Bootstrap/Bootswatch Sass variables before the theme compiles, giving much deeper control.
_quarto.yml:
format:
  html:
    theme:
      - morph        # base Bootswatch theme
      - custom.scss  # your overrides on top
Do not mix css: and theme: [morph, custom.scss] — use one approach only.

title: How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
How to Style a Quarto Blog from Default
text:
Based on hard-won lessons from this session:

Wrong selector for banner height — .title-block-banner alone does not control the rendered height; use .quarto-title-banner. Using the wrong selector adds a second padding on top of the theme default, making it taller.
Setting $body-bg kills theme backgrounds — Morph and other themed backgrounds are overwritten by $body-bg. Omit it to preserve the theme’s native background.
css: is ignored when using SCSS overlay — If _quarto.yml uses theme: [morph, custom.scss], any css: styles.css line is silently ignored. All CSS rules must live in custom.scss.
Broad div { color: ... !important } breaks navbar/footer — Targeting div globally will override navbar and banner text colors. Scope selectors to main, .content, or .page-content.
Hard refresh is required — quarto preview caches stylesheets. Always use Ctrl+Shift+R after changing .scss files, or restart the preview server.
Padding rules cascade unexpectedly — Adding padding to the wrong parent (e.g., .quarto-title-meta, .title-metadata-section) stacks on top of existing padding rather than replacing it. Inspect with browser DevTools first to identify the exact element before adding rules.

title: About Ivan
text:
Hi!
This is my personal blog about my profession and hobbies. Currently I am specializing on computational biology and data science in biomedical research conducting a postdoc project at Novartis. My gate computer languages are R and bash, but I also have some experience with Python as well as with html and other relevant tools like Quarto, Github and AI assistants. My biological background spans through several major areas currently converging on cell and molecular biology, systems biology and omics. I am highly interested in understanding tissue regeneration through mitigating the inflammation and fibrosis as well as other aspects of cell signaling and senescence.
In my free time I prefer to learn how to program, foreign languages, listen to and play music on guitar. I do yoga daily and try to hike outside of my town every month. Besides acquiring hard skills I develop soft skills through participation in Toastmasters meetings, local Ski and Mountain club and occassionally writing blog posts.
My passion is transforming clinical and biological data into actionable insights by exploratory analyses, challenging biological hypotheses and performing data visualization. For further details on this please visit my Github and LinkedIn profiles.

title: Welcome To My Blog
text:
This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

Since this post doesn’t specify an explicit image, the first image in the post will be used in the listing page of posts.
